name: TAO 4h Failed Breakdown Hold Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: a breakdown attempt fails, support is reclaimed and held, and continuation is entered only once the hold confirms that trend pressure has reasserted
thesis: Cycle 75 explicitly includes failed breakdown reclaim that must hold before continuation entry. TAO 4h is the best-fit lane because it is the structurally different asset in the basket and still carries live trend-up contamination. The edge is not anti-trend mean reversion. It is buying the failed breakdown only after the market proves it can hold reclaimed support.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h failed-breakdown holds remain too sparse or reclaim-hold sequences fail before continuation, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks below local support, rejects the breakdown, reclaims support, holds it, and then resumes in the dominant direction.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In a trend-up lane, failed breakdowns can trap late shorts and become fuel for trend resumption. The hold requirement filters some one-bar noise without turning the setup into a ceremonial sparse chain.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it is the only lane in the basket where failed breakdown continuation has clear structural support from the regime.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The hold may still come too late after the move, converting the setup into local exhaustion buying.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries work but drawdown is too large, pull the first partial closer before changing the hold requirement.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower count than ETH/BTC reclaim structures, but better asymmetry when the trend leg truly resumes.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears density and failed-breakdown hold continuation shows cleaner asymmetry than the recent bad 1h ideas.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or repeated reclaim-hold entries that fail back through support within 1-2 bars.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable because TAO trend contamination should create tradable failed breakdowns

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit trend-up lane for failed breakdown hold continuation.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h produces enough reclaim-hold resumptions with positive first-target expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h reclaim-hold entries keep failing back through support.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with a few strong continuation runners.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same failed-breakdown hold works in a less directional major.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h preserves reclaim-hold behavior even if the continuation is smaller.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h turns it into weak chop noise instead of continuation.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Smaller, more balance-like reclaim continuations than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Useful control to see whether the thesis is too dependent on TAO trend structure.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still prints orderly reclaim-hold continuation despite chop.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the concept needs trend contamination.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Sparse and smaller reclaim continuations than TAO.

long: Next bar low remains >= DONCHIAN_LOW_20 - 0.12 * ATR_14
short: Next bar high remains <= DONCHIAN_HIGH_20 + 0.12 * ATR_14
